Credit VivoLive credit. Clear path.
DashboardFindingsMessagesMonthlyVaultUpload

Phased strategy

Dispute Center

Credit Vivo does not dispute every possible error at once. The portal uses staged rounds so the strongest, best-supported issues go first and weaker items wait for evidence or response results.

Ready for Round 1

3Strongest issues with clear support

Hold Queue

9Needs proof, response, or manual review

Pending Responses

0Starts after certified mail or portal submission

Escalation Review

0Only after weak or no response

Dispute Rounds

Prep

Day 0-3

Setup and proof check

Upload report, ID, proof of address, and any letters from collectors.

Internal rule: No disputes leave until identity, report source, account grouping, and evidence strength are checked.

Current
Round 1

Day 3-7

Strongest first

Start with the clearest possible errors, not every issue at once.

Internal rule: Send only high-confidence, high-impact disputes such as duplicate debt, wrong balance, wrong status, mixed-file identity, or missing original creditor on collections.

Preview ready
Wait Window

Day 30-45

Response tracking

Track bureau and furnisher responses before sending the next round.

Internal rule: Do not stack repetitive disputes while the first investigation window is open. Log results and compare response documents to the raw report.

Scheduled
Round 2

After responses

Next evidence-backed group

Continue with issues that still appear inaccurate or unverifiable.

Internal rule: Use the response results to decide which remaining accounts deserve a second dispute, direct furnisher dispute, or document request.

Held
Round 3

After Round 2 review

Direct furnisher or validation follow-up

Use direct follow-up only when the account still needs proof, itemization, or correction.

Internal rule: Send account-specific direct furnisher disputes or validation requests only when the evidence supports it.

Locked
Round 4+

After failed verification

Escalation only when justified

Escalate only if the response does not meaningfully address the issue.

Internal rule: Consider CFPB, state regulator, or attorney review only for unresolved, documented, high-impact issues. Do not escalate every item.

Locked

Send First

  • Duplicate same-debt reporting
  • Balance/status conflicts with clear proof
  • Wrong identity or mixed-file information
  • Collection missing original creditor or ownership support

Hold For Later

  • Low-confidence parser fields
  • Items missing customer proof
  • Recently disputed accounts still inside response window
  • Low-impact issues that may distract from stronger errors

Do Not Send

  • Accurate negative items with no factual error
  • Generic template disputes with no account-specific reason
  • Repeated disputes before reviewing prior responses
  • Anything that needs attorney review before action

Customer View vs Backend Engine

Customers see simple progress: upload received, first dispute package ready, waiting for response, next review scheduled. The backend keeps the deeper scanner logic, raw report comparison, field confidence, response audit, and escalation rules hidden from the customer portal.

Launch status: Portal dispute planning is available. Mailing, CFPB/state complaints, and attorney communication stay disabled until customer authorization, supporting documents, delivery tracking, response review, and compliance review are connected.
Credit Vivo

Results vary. Credit Vivo does not guarantee removals, approvals, or credit score increases. You may dispute inaccurate credit report information directly with the credit bureaus at no cost.
